title: [CI:DOCS] Add Japanese locale and translation of index
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Hi, team. 
Thank you for all the efforts in this great project.
I believe adding locales can help podman to spread for non English speakers as discussed  https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/11758. Especially in Japan, the Japanese translation of Podman in Action is going to be published on Sep, 2023. (I'm a part of the team for this translation.)

The translation of docs.podman.io also would be quite helpful for non English speaking users. To begin with, we would like to add directory for locales and translated index.

I generated files by [sphinx-intl](https://sphinx-intl.readthedocs.io/en/master/quickstart.html) and translated it into Japanese. Sorry for including some huge files. If there is another proper way to add locales, please let me know. Thank you.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

![Screenshot 2023-08-30 at 13 10 39](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/41185206/53bd7652-3782-400e-aa15-85a88df1e43f)

comments.body:
Thanks for the review.
> Some man-pages have trailing whitespaces it needs to fixed. See validate test in CI

Let me check and fix later.

> Also could you clarify what are these binary .mo files and do we need that in repo ?

Sorry, we don't need to include them in repo. These .mo files were generated when running `make html -e SPHINXOPTS='-D language="ja"'`, so I will delete them from this PR.
Thank you @nishipy, this is an impressive undertaking. I am not qualified to review, but could I ask you to please squash your commits? Kind thanks.

Thank you! But it's not quite right yet. First, there's an unnecessary merge commit; and second, the commit message on d045ba941 now includes inapplicable (and confusing) history. For the benefit of a future maintainer reading through the git history, could you please remove the merge commit, and simplify the commit message? Thank you again.
I see. Thank you for pointing it out. Let me fix it.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *nishipy* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@yuokada126 Please review, since you understand the Japanese.
The Japanese part looks good to me.
